tool_args.label: NYCT ridership decline and recovery narrative
tool_args.raw_text: Due to COVID-19 travel limitations and a significant reduction in employment-based trips, all transit services in the New York area experienced steep ridership declines from late March 2020 through early 2021. Between February and March 2021, the rate of decline slowed in all agencies and on all modes of transportation, and in March - November 2021, a new period of growth began.

payload.claim_text: Due to COVID-19 travel limitations and a significant reduction in employment-based trips, all transit services in the New York area experienced steep ridership declines from late March 2020 through early 2021. Between February and March 2021, the rate of decline slowed in all agencies and on all modes of transportation, and in March - November 2021, a new period of growth began.

tool_args.label: Omicron variant ridership retreat Dec 2021 - Jan 2022
tool_args.raw_text: December 2021 and January 2022 saw ridership retreat under the Omicron variant wave of infection across the region. Ridership has since rebounded in February – July 2022 back up to November 2021 pre-Omicron peak levels

payload.claim_text: December 2021 and January 2022 saw ridership retreat under the Omicron variant wave of infection across the region. Ridership has since rebounded in February – July 2022 back up to November 2021 pre-Omicron peak levels

tool_args.label: NYCT overtime overrun due to vacancy coverage
